steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says rising comic book prices had “the counterintuitive effect of ______ demand.” Normally price increases reduce demand, but here buyers flooded the market. The blank must describe what the price rise caused — a word meaning triggered or set off.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — monetizing: To monetize means to convert something into a source of revenue. The blank describes what happened to demand, not whether anyone profited — this word does not fit the causal structure. ✗
  • Choice B — precipitating: To precipitate means to cause or trigger something suddenly. “Rising prices precipitating a surge in demand” captures the causal chain: the price increase triggered buyers to flood the market. ✓
  • Choice C — stabilizing: To stabilize means to make something steady. The passage describes buyers suddenly thronging the market — a spike, not stability. This contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — exploiting: To exploit means to take unfair advantage of something. Rising prices did not take advantage of demand; the buyers are the ones responding to opportunity — the direction of the action is wrong. ✗
That is the correct answer.
